phisher.subtitle: Automated Phishing Simulation & Human Risk Management
phisher.lede: Deploy realistic phishing simulations across your organization, identify vulnerable users, and build cyber resilience with targeted training interventions.
phisher.caps_title: Everything you need to manage human risk
phisher.feat_campaigns_desc: Schedule and deploy realistic phishing simulations with 1,000+ customizable templates targeting your organization's specific threat profile.
phisher.feat_targeting_desc: Personalize campaigns by department, role, location, and risk profile for maximum behavioral insight.
phisher.feat_dashboard_desc: Track click rates, reporting rates, and user vulnerability scores with executive-ready dashboards.
phisher.feat_triggers_desc: Automatically enroll at-risk users in targeted security awareness training when they fail simulations.
phisher.feat_score_desc: Each user receives a dynamic risk score based on simulation performance, training completion, and behavioral indicators.
phisher.step_configure_desc: Select from 1,000+ realistic templates, choose your target audience, and schedule your simulation window.
phisher.step_launch_desc: PhishER automatically delivers personalized phishing attempts across your organization with full tracking.
phisher.step_analyze_desc: Review detailed reports showing who clicked, who reported, and which departments need attention.
phisher.step_remediate_desc: Users who fail are automatically enrolled in targeted microlearning modules to address specific vulnerabilities.
phisher.cta_sub: See how PhishER measures and reduces human risk across your organization.
phisher.meta_desc: Automated Phishing Simulation & Human Risk Management. Deploy realistic phishing simulations, identify vulnerable users, and build cyber resilience with targeted training interventions.

bygrc.why_title: Compliance shouldn't be a fire drill

bygrc.before_d: Manual processes. Fragmented documents. Gaps discovered only during audits.
bygrc.after_d: Living documents. AI search. Real-time coverage across every framework from one platform.
bygrc.always_d: When the law changes, your platform updates — not your workload.

bygrc.pillar3_d: Treeview navigation and dashboards showing compliance status in real time.

about.meta_desc: Founded in crisis, built for resilience. Brandvakt — the digital sentinels of our age. Offices across Africa and LATAM; partnerships across four continents.
about.subtitle: From the ancient city guards who watched for fire, to the cybersecurity professionals protecting your organization today.
about.etym_def: Swedish, noun. From brand (fire) + vakt (watch, guard).
about.etym_desc: The sentinel who ensures the fire burns smoothly, doesn't spread, contains it if something goes wrong, and ensures the safety of everyone.
about.story_quote: "The word Brandvakt means 'Firewatch'. The Brandvakt ensures the fire burns smoothly, doesn't spread, and ensures the safety of everyone — that is how we see our role in the digital world."
about.story_p: Founded during the pandemic, Brandvakt is a trusted partner for financial services, telecommunications, and energy sectors — using AI to accelerate threat detection, automate incident response, and anticipate risks before they materialize.
about.tl_2020_d: Digital threats surged. Organizations were unprepared. Brandvakt launched.
about.tl_2021_d: First international office. DRC presence established to serve Central African enterprises.
about.tl_2022_d: Angola and Brazil opened simultaneously — spanning the South Atlantic corridor.
about.tl_2023_d: West Africa expansion. Offices established in Abidjan and Bamako to serve francophone enterprises.
about.tl_2024_d: Q4 2024. Recognized cybersecurity training programs built by active industry practitioners.
about.tl_2025_d: AI-native partnership with HomoDeus integrated into the full service portfolio.
about.std_sub: Proven, defensible frameworks. No proprietary reinvention.
about.std_nist_d: Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover — the five pillars of resilient security.
about.std_iso_d: International standard for establishing and maintaining an effective ISMS.
about.std_cis_d: 18 prioritized controls defending against the most prevalent attack techniques.
about.std_mitre_d: Adversary tactics and techniques powering our threat detection and hunting operations.
about.val1_d: Reputation is an asset. Every interaction reinforces it.
about.val2_d: We avoid conflicts of interest. Quality of work is non-negotiable.
about.val3_d: No bribery, no corruption, no fraud. No exceptions.
about.csr_sub: Social, economic, and environmental accountability are embedded in operations — not appended as policy.
about.csr1_d: Security knowledge should not be a privilege. We train young professionals to close that gap.
about.csr2_d: Merit-driven. Participation open to all who share the mission.
about.csr3_d: Economic, social, and environmental balance — across every process.
about.cta_sub: Architect, implement, and manage your security capabilities with us.

csma.meta_desc: Free AI-generated cybersecurity maturity assessment — answer 18 questions across NIST CSF, CIS Controls and ISO 27001 and receive an executive diagnosis, gap analysis, and a 12-month remediation roadmap.
csma.hero_sub: Answer 18 questions and receive an AI-generated executive diagnosis — complete with gap analysis, commercial proposal, and a 12-month remediation roadmap. In under 5 minutes.
csma.dom_identify_desc: Asset management, risk identification, supply chain, and organizational context (NIST ID · CIS 1-3-15-18 · ISO A.5).
csma.dom_protect_desc: Access control, MFA, encryption, patch management, and security awareness training (NIST PR · CIS 5-7-14 · ISO A.8).
csma.dom_detect_desc: Continuous monitoring, centralized logging, anomaly detection, and threat intelligence (NIST DE · CIS 8 · ISO A.8.15).
csma.dom_respond_desc: Incident response plan, designated team, communication procedures, and lessons learned (NIST RS · CIS 17 · ISO A.5.26).
csma.dom_recover_desc: Backup integrity, business continuity, disaster recovery, and defined RTO/RPO targets (NIST RC · CIS 11 · ISO A.5.29).
csma.q_identify_0: Is there an up-to-date, complete inventory of all IT assets — hardware, software, data, and cloud services?
csma.q_identify_1: Are security risks formally identified, documented, and prioritized by business process?
csma.q_identify_2: Is there a data classification policy implemented, communicated, and actively monitored?
csma.q_identify_3: Are third-party suppliers and service providers assessed for security risks prior to and during engagement?
csma.q_protect_0: Is least-privilege access control implemented and periodically reviewed across all critical systems?
csma.q_protect_1: Is MFA active for all remote access, corporate email, and sensitive systems?
csma.q_protect_2: Are security patches applied within defined SLAs based on vulnerability criticality?
csma.q_protect_3: Is sensitive data encrypted in transit (TLS) and at rest, with formal key management?
csma.q_protect_4: Do all employees receive formal security awareness training at least once per year?
csma.q_detect_0: Is there continuous security event monitoring with automated alerting — SIEM, EDR, or equivalent?
csma.q_detect_1: Are access and critical activity logs collected centrally, integrity-protected, and reviewed regularly?
csma.q_detect_2: Is there a formal process for anomaly detection, suspicious behavior analysis, and IoC identification?
csma.q_respond_0: Is there a formal incident response plan — documented, leadership-approved, and tested in the past 12 months?
csma.q_respond_1: Is there a formally designated team or individual responsible for coordinating security incident response?
csma.q_respond_2: Are communication procedures to regulators, customers, and stakeholders mapped and tested for incidents?
csma.q_recover_0: Are backups performed regularly, stored securely, and tested to verify actual recoverability?
csma.q_recover_1: Is there a BCP and DR plan — documented, approved by leadership, and tested in the past 12 months?
csma.q_recover_2: Are RTO and RPO formally defined and communicated for all critical business systems?
csma.cap_title: Where should we send your report?
csma.cap_sub: Your AI-generated security diagnosis, gap analysis, and commercial proposal will be tailored to your organization. No spam — just your report.
csma.cap_err: Please fill in name, email, and company.
csma.cap_consent: By submitting, you agree to be contacted by a Brandvakt specialist about your results and to your data being processed in line with our
csma.loading_sub: Our AI is analyzing your security posture across all 5 domains
csma.loader_0: Cross-referencing 18 controls against NIST · CIS · ISO 27001...
csma.loader_1: Computing maturity score for each security domain...
csma.loader_2: Identifying critical gaps and risk exposure...
csma.cta_sub: Our specialists turn your diagnosis into an executable security roadmap.
csma.fb_diagnosis:
With an overall score of {{pct}}% ({{level}} level), {{company}} presents a security posture that reflects uneven control maturity across the five NIST CSF domains. The {{worst}} domain — scoring lowest in this assessment — represents the most acute point of operational exposure, where informal or absent controls create systemic vulnerability to both opportunistic and targeted threats.

The {{crit}} critical gaps (score 0–1) represent immediate regulatory and operational risk. In jurisdictions subject to LGPD or GDPR, the absence of documented controls in these areas constitutes a compliance deficit that audit bodies can act upon without a triggering incident. The reputational and financial exposure in a breach scenario is compounded by the absence of a tested incident response capability.

The highest-leverage immediate actions are: formalizing the asset inventory and classification scheme, implementing MFA universally (the single highest-return security control per CISA and CIS data), and testing the incident response plan against a tabletop scenario. These three actions, executed within 90 days, would elevate the overall score by an estimated 8–12 percentage points.

Resolving the identified control gaps positions the organization not merely to reduce risk, but to compete for enterprise contracts, pass vendor security questionnaires, and qualify for preferred cyber insurance terms. For organizations pursuing ISO 27001 certification, this assessment represents the gap analysis that typically costs $15,000–$40,000 from a Big-4 advisory firm.
csma.fb_phase0: The immediate priority is to address the {{crit}} controls currently at score 0–1. Begin with formalizing the asset inventory (CIS Control 1-2), implementing MFA across all administrative and remote access (CIS 6.3 / ISO A.8.5), and establishing a documented incident response procedure (NIST RS.RP). These three actions alone materially reduce the probability of a successful attack and are prerequisites for any subsequent compliance work.
csma.fb_phase1: Months 4 through 8 should focus on elevating scores in the {{worst}} domain from their current baseline to a minimum Defined level (3/5). This requires documented procedures with owner accountability, quarterly review cycles, and implementation of centralized logging with defined retention periods. Security awareness training must shift from ad-hoc to structured — scheduled, tracked, and tied to role-based risk profiles.
csma.fb_phase2: The final quarter positions the organization for ISO 27001 certification readiness and a sustainable Managed maturity posture. This includes a formal management review cycle, internal audit capability, supplier risk assessment integration, and continuous improvement metrics tracked at the executive level. Achieving this state eliminates most due diligence objections in enterprise sales cycles and M&A processes.
csma.fb_proposal:
Your current security posture at {{pct}}% reflects a transitional organization — one that has made initial investments in security but has not yet systematized its controls into a defensible, auditable framework. The {{gaps}} priority gaps identified represent both operational risk and a missed opportunity to differentiate in regulated markets.

Brandvakt Academy's Compliance Suite directly addresses your governance and framework gaps, providing automated controls mapping against ISO 27001, LGPD, and GDPR.
